task_completion.description: GIMP is open displaying a landscape image
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is visible with a landscape photo loaded in the main canvas.

task_completion.description: The Gradient tool is selected in the GIMP toolbox
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Gradient tool icon is highlighted or actively selected in the GIMP toolbox panel.

task_completion.description: A horizontal gradient is applied from left to right
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image canvas shows a color or opacity gradient that changes smoothly along the horizontal axis (left to right).

task_completion.description: The gradient covers at least 70% of the image width
task_completion.visual_evidence: The gradient effect spans across the vast majority of the image horizontally, rather than being a small localized gradient.

task_completion.description: The image is significantly modified by the gradient
task_completion.visual_evidence: A large portion of the original landscape image is visibly altered, covered, or tinted by the gradient fill.

integrity.description: Agent used the GIMP GUI to apply the gradient
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GIMP interface, tool selection, and canvas interaction, rather than terminal commands like ImageMagick.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with GIMP, not a different program
integrity.visual_evidence: The application being used is GIMP, not an image viewer or web browser showing a pre-made image.

integrity.description: Agent did not use a script to bypass GUI interaction
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal or GIMP Python-Fu/Script-Fu console is used to run a script that automatically applies the gradient.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task is performed in GIMP using an 800x600 landscape image. The gradient must be applied horizontally from left to right. While the description says 'entire image', the actual requirement is that the gradient covers at least 70% of the image width. The image must be significantly modified (at least 50% of pixels changed) with smooth transitions.
